payload.summary: Batch B complete — 6 methodology posts reviewed with dual AR + EM rubrics. AR mean 8.58 (vs Batch A 7.2 — genre advantage, not quality uplift). EM mean 6.32 (vs Batch A 4.4 — genuine improvement from linked artifacts, but self-referential sourcing persists). 3 pass-with-notes, 3 revise. Persistent issues: '173 nations' unsourced across batches, self-referential sourcing ecosystem, CTA in reflective posts without mode-shift signaling.

methodology.ar_rubric: docs/adversarial-register-rubric.md — 3-dimension weighted scoring (dialogue mode 0.40, stance markers 0.35, attribution pattern 0.25)
methodology.em_rubric: docs/ethical-marketing-rubric.md — 5-dimension weighted scoring (substantiation 0.30, transparency 0.25, proportionality 0.20, respect_for_audience 0.15, accountability 0.10)
methodology.scoring_context: 6 posts reviewed via agent subagent applying identical dual rubric. Post content accessed via GitHub API (raw markdown). Each post scored independently.

defensibility_findings.finding: Five named gaps strengthen defensibility but the gap list may create a false sense of completeness — reader may infer these represent ALL limitations rather than a curated subset.
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Add a sentence acknowledging these represent the most significant gaps, not an exhaustive list.

defensibility_findings.finding: Wolfram verification (37/37) provides strong statistical checking, but interpretive claims built on those statistics (e.g., 'HRCB discriminates meaningfully') involve judgments Wolfram cannot verify.
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Clarify that Wolfram verified computational accuracy, not interpretive validity.

source_issues: Methodology, API, OpenAPI spec, and GitHub findings all linked — strong sourcing infrastructure
External validation sources (psychometric standards, human rights measurement literature) absent — all sources self-referential to Observatory ecosystem
concerns_em: All linked sources remain within unratified.org / Observatory ecosystem — no independent external sources cited for psychometric standards or validation benchmarks
Known-groups validity test lacks independent ground truth — the 'known groups' were presumably defined by the same system
batch_b_results.summary: Strongest Batch B post on both rubrics; names its own gaps explicitly, sources its claims with linked artifacts, and operates in deliberative mode — EM concern limited to ecosystem-internal sourcing.

defensibility_findings.finding: Taxonomy derived from 3 exchanges (31 rounds) with a single model (Gemini). Sample size limits generalizability — these 7 types may represent Gemini-specific confabulation patterns rather than universal LLM types. Post title ('When AI Hallucinates') implies generality.
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Qualify the title or add a note that the taxonomy derives from Gemini exchanges specifically.

defensibility_findings.finding: Detection difficulty ranking (EASY to HARD) presented without explicit criteria for the ranking.
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Add brief criteria or examples for each difficulty level.

defensibility_findings.finding: Self-observation paradox documented but not resolved — the post observes that an LLM analyzing LLM confabulation faces the same risk, but offers no mitigation.
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Name a specific mitigation strategy, even if partial.

source_issues: All transcripts linked in GitHub — strong primary source availability
No citations to existing confabulation/hallucination taxonomy literature (e.g., Ji et al. 2023, Huang et al. 2023) — taxonomy appears constructed de novo without positioning against prior work
concerns_em: Generalizability claim implicit in title scope exceeds the evidence base (3 Gemini exchanges)
No engagement with existing LLM hallucination literature — taxonomy presented as if novel without checking whether types map to known categories
batch_b_results.summary: High AR — charitable toward Gemini, documents own paradox; EM concern centers on implied generalizability from a narrow sample and absence of prior-work citations.

defensibility_findings.finding: All 5 sources link to unratified.org. For a methodology post describing 6 analytical techniques, complete absence of external references represents a substantiation gap. Techniques either derive from established methods (cite origins) or represent novel constructs (state novelty explicitly).
defensibility_findings.recommendation: For each technique: cite the prior work it derives from, OR explicitly label it as a novel construct developed within this project.

defensibility_findings.finding: Single-rater limitation acknowledged but the post describes methodology as if validated. Gap between 'we use these techniques' and 'these techniques produce reliable results' remains unaddressed.
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Add explicit language distinguishing operational use from validated methodology.

defensibility_findings.finding: Internet-grounding named as critical dependency but post does not quantify how results degrade without it — makes the dependency claim unfalsifiable.
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Describe at least one concrete example where internet-grounding changed a result.

source_issues: Zero external citations for any of 6 described techniques
Knock-on analysis lacks attribution to prior futures/foresight methodology
E-prime attributed culturally but not cited to Bourland (1965) or Korzybski (1933)
violations_em: Self-referential-only sourcing for a methodology post describing 6 analytical techniques — a methodology post functions as a credibility claim for the entire site, and its sourcing standard should exceed individual advocacy posts
concerns_em: Techniques presented without distinguishing adapted-from-prior-work vs. novel constructs
Single-rater limitation acknowledged but not reflected in confidence language about techniques' effectiveness
batch_b_results.summary: Strong AR register (deliberative, self-critical) but EM score dragged down by fully self-referential sourcing on a methodology post that implicitly supports all other posts' credibility.

defensibility_findings.finding: '173 nations ratified' appears unsourced. Previously flagged in Batch A. Repetition without adding a source compounds the concern — citation-less repeated assertion pattern across batches.
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Add UN Treaty Collection link. This claim now needs a canonical source given its frequency across the series.

defensibility_findings.finding: Advocacy CTA at end shifts from methodology reflection to policy advocacy without signaling. Reader moves from epistemic self-examination to a normative claim without a mode-shift marker.
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Add explicit transition language (e.g., 'Moving from analysis to advocacy:') or separate the CTA into a distinct section.

defensibility_findings.finding: Sources mostly self-referential (4/5 internal, 1 OHCHR). For a post about analysis boundaries, the external reference pool should exceed the internal one.
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Add at least 2-3 external references for claims about futures analysis methodology.

source_issues: 1 OHCHR external link — sole external source
'173 nations ratified' unsourced (previously flagged in Batch A)
Confidence degradation curve presented without external methodological reference
violations_em: Unsourced '173 nations' claim repeated from Batch A without remediation — persistence of flagged claims across batches indicates systemic sourcing gap
concerns_em: Unmarked mode shift from methodology reflection to policy advocacy CTA
batch_b_results.summary: Self-aware epistemic post undercut by repeating the unsourced '173 nations' claim from Batch A and an unmarked shift from reflective methodology to advocacy CTA.

defensibility_findings.finding: Three unsourced quantitative claims: '173 countries' (repeated from Batch A), '10.9 million people' (CBO attributed but not linked), '$990 billion in cuts' (unsourced). A meta-reflection post that critiques its own accuracy while containing unsourced statistics demonstrates the exact problem it describes.
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Link CBO report for 10.9M claim. Source the $990B claim. Add UN Treaty Collection for 173 countries.

defensibility_findings.finding: CTA ('Send a letter to your senator') appears in a post whose primary mode is self-critical reflection. Juxtaposition of 'here are our limitations' with 'now act based on our output' creates unresolved tension.
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Either remove the CTA from this post or explicitly address the tension: 'Despite these limitations, we believe the factual case stands because...'

defensibility_findings.finding: References to confabulation taxonomy provide internal cross-linking but no external hallucination detection literature.
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Add 1-2 external references to LLM reliability literature.

source_issues: '10.9 million people (CBO)' attributed but not linked
Internal cross-references present but no external sources for reliability claims
violations_em: Three unsourced quantitative claims in a post explicitly about accuracy and honesty — the self-assessment genre creates heightened substantiation expectations
concerns_em: CTA in self-critical reflection creates unresolved tension between skepticism and action request
Dependency question raised but not resolved — 'what if Claude gets it wrong?' lacks a concrete answer
batch_b_results.summary: High AR — genuinely self-critical, names what Claude gets wrong; EM dragged down by the irony of unsourced statistics in a post about accuracy, plus unresolved CTA-in-self-criticism tension.

defensibility_findings.finding: GEO (Grounded Epistemic Override) documented as working in-context but not cross-session. Finding presented as general LLM limitation, but evidence base consists of Gemini sessions only.
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Scope GEO finding explicitly to Gemini. Note whether similar patterns observed in Claude or flag as untested.

defensibility_findings.finding: Gemini's adversarial test paragraph against Observatory methodology documented but the post does not describe the outcome.
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Include the outcome: did the test reveal something genuine, or did it fail to find a weakness?

defensibility_findings.finding: Evaluation JSONs linked in GitHub — strong artifact availability. However, Gemini's scoring methodology not described, making the 'scored us back' claim partially opaque.
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Summarize Gemini's scoring criteria so the reader can assess reciprocal evaluation rigor.

source_issues: All evaluation JSONs linked in GitHub — strong primary source infrastructure
No external references to cross-model evaluation or AI peer review literature
GEO finding lacks citation to relevant in-context learning or grounding literature
concerns_em: GEO generalizability implied beyond evidence base (single model)
Gemini's scoring methodology not described — reader cannot assess reciprocal evaluation rigor
batch_b_results.summary: Highest AR in Batch B — genuinely deliberative, documents collaboration and confabulation with equal weight; EM strong with artifacts linked, primary concerns around GEO generalizability and undescribed reciprocal methodology.

comparison_to_batch_a.ar_delta: +1.38 (genre advantage — methodology posts naturally deliberative, not quality uplift)
comparison_to_batch_a.em_delta: +1.92 (genuine improvement from linked artifacts; self-referential sourcing persists as weaker form of Batch A zero-citation pattern)
comparison_to_batch_a.substantiation_delta: +2.85 (biggest improvement — posts link artifacts, but external citations still scarce)
comparison_to_batch_a.transparency_delta: +3.17 (AI disclosure stronger, self-description better aligned with actual content)

systemic_findings_batch_b.finding: Self-referential sourcing ecosystem. Batch A had zero citations. Batch B has citations, but they overwhelmingly link back to unratified.org, Observatory GitHub, or other project artifacts. A methodology post that cites only its own ecosystem for validation creates a closed epistemic loop — the evidence for the method's quality comes from the method itself.
systemic_findings_batch_b.remediation: For each described technique in methodology posts: cite the prior work it derives from, OR explicitly label it as a novel construct. Add at least 2-3 external references per methodology post.

systemic_findings_batch_b.finding: Persistent '173 nations ratified' claim unsourced across both batches. Flagged in Batch A, reappears in Batch B Posts 4 and 5 without remediation. The repetition suggests either Batch A findings did not reach the content pipeline, or the pipeline lacks a mechanism to propagate sourcing requirements to already-published claims.
systemic_findings_batch_b.remediation: Add UN Treaty Collection canonical link. Propagate to all posts where this claim appears.

systemic_findings_batch_b.finding: CTA in reflective posts without mode-shift signaling. Posts 4 and 5 embed advocacy CTAs inside methodology/self-critical reflections. The mode shift from analysis to advocacy occurs without signaling — a proportionality concern specific to Batch B that did not appear in Batch A (where entire posts were advocacy).
systemic_findings_batch_b.remediation: Add explicit transition markers when shifting from reflection to advocacy, or separate CTAs into distinct sections.

systemic_findings_batch_b.finding: Genre advantage on AR. Methodology posts score ~1.4 points higher than voter guides — reflects genre rather than quality improvement. A methodology post has no opponent to be adversarial toward, so it naturally registers as deliberative. Batch B AR scores should not create complacency about voter guide AR scores.
systemic_findings_batch_b.note: Informational — no remediation needed. Context for cross-batch comparison.

remediation_priority_batch_b: 1. Source '173 nations' claim across all posts — UN Treaty Collection canonical link (fixes SB-2, B4-F1, B5-F1)
2. Add external references to methodology posts — cite prior work for each technique or label as novel (fixes SB-1, B3-F1; expected substantiation lift +2.0)
3. Source CBO and budget claims in honest-assessment post (fixes B5-F1)
4. Signal mode shifts when methodology posts transition to advocacy CTAs (fixes SB-3, B4-F2, B5-F2)
5. Scope generalizability claims — confabulation title implies all-AI, GEO finding implies all-models (fixes B2-F1, B6-F1)

cumulative_progress.remaining: C: 10 technical, D: 5 observatory, E: 1 psychology-authored, F: 1 welcome
cumulative_progress.next_batch: Batch C (10 technical posts) — expected to score similarly to Batch B on AR (methodology/technical genre) with variable EM depending on sourcing infrastructure

claims: All 6 Batch B posts reviewed with dual AR + EM rubrics
Post content accessed via GitHub API raw markdown
AR and EM scores computed using published rubrics (docs/adversarial-register-rubric.md, docs/ethical-marketing-rubric.md)
Genre advantage explicitly noted — Batch B AR scores not directly comparable to Batch A
epistemic_flags: Single reviewer (psychology-agent). No inter-rater reliability established for Batch B scoring.
Scores based on post content accessed via GitHub API. If content changed since access, scores may not reflect current state.

AR scores for methodology posts benefit from genre advantage (no opponent = higher deliberative score). Direct comparison of Batch B AR mean (8.58) to Batch A AR mean (7.2) overstates quality improvement.
EM rubric extends FTC substantiation doctrine to non-commercial advocacy content. Legal enforcement threshold remains higher for advocacy than commercial speech.
Self-referential sourcing scored as partial substantiation (better than zero, weaker than external). This judgment call affects Posts 1, 2, 3, and 6 materially.
